---
name: terminology
description: VS Code documentation terminology glossary. Use when writing or reviewing content to ensure consistent terminology, capitalization, and word choice.
---
# VS Code Terminology Glossary

Use this glossary when writing or reviewing VS Code documentation, blog posts, and release notes. Consistent terminology improves readability and searchability.

## Product names

| Correct | Incorrect | Notes |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| Visual Studio Code | Visual Studio code, VSCode, VS code | Spell out on first reference in an article body |
| VS Code | VSCode, vscode, vs code | Use after first reference |
| GitHub Copilot | Copilot (first ref), copilot | Spell out "GitHub Copilot" on first reference |
| Extension Marketplace | extension marketplace, marketplace | Capitalize as proper noun |
| Command Palette | command palette | Capitalize as proper noun |
| Settings editor | Settings Editor | Not a proper noun, sentence case |

## UI elements

Use these canonical names and capitalize them as proper nouns:

| Element | Notes |
|---------|-------|
| Activity Bar | Vertical bar on the side |
| Primary Side Bar | Left panel (default) |
| Secondary Side Bar | Optional right panel |
| Editor | The code editing area |
| Editor Group | A set of editor tabs |
| Panel | Bottom area (Terminal, Output, etc.) |
| Status Bar | Bottom bar |
| Title Bar | Top bar with menus |
| Command Palette | `kb(workbench.action.showCommands)` |
| Quick Pick | Dropdown selection UI |
| Minimap | Code overview on editor side |
| Breadcrumbs | Navigation path above editor |
| Explorer | File tree in Side Bar |

## Action verbs

| Use | Do not use | Context |
|-----|-----------|---------|
| Select | Click, click on, press | For UI elements (buttons, menus, options) |
| Enter | Type in, input | For text entry |
| Open | Launch, fire up | For files, editors, dialogs |
| Run | Execute | For commands, tasks |
| Go to | Navigate to | For locations in the UI |
| might | may | For possibility |
| can | is able to | For capability |
| use | utilize, leverage | For employing a feature |

## Banned words

Never use these words in documentation:

* simply, just, easy, easily
* obviously, of course, clearly, naturally
* basically, actually, really
* utilize, leverage
* delve, crucial
* a myriad of
* etc. (use "and so on" or list all items)

## Technical terms

| Correct | Incorrect |
|---------|-----------|
| IntelliSense | Intellisense, intellisense, auto-complete |
| Emmet | emmet, EMMET |
| Git | git, GIT |
| GitHub | Github, github |
| Node.js | NodeJS, node.js, Nodejs |
| TypeScript | Typescript, typescript |
| JavaScript | Javascript, javascript |
| npm | NPM, Npm |
| Markdown | markdown, MarkDown |
| JSON | json, Json |
| YAML | yaml, Yaml |
| HTML | html, Html |
| CSS | css, Css |
| Python | python |
| Copilot Chat | copilot chat |
| Copilot Edits | copilot edits |

## Abbreviations

* Spell out on first reference, then abbreviate: "Language Server Protocol (LSP)".
* Common abbreviations that do not need spelling out: API, URL, UI, HTML, CSS, JSON, YAML.
* Do not capitalize the spelled-out form unless it is a proper noun.
